modelResponses.text:
The task completed successfully! Here's a full summary of what was done: --- ## ✅ Repository Change Applied & PR Created ### 📁 Repository **`octo-org/benchmark-demo-repo`** — branch: `main` --- ### 📝 Changed Files | File | Summary | |------|---------| | `README.md` | Corrected setup typo and clarified benchmark run command formatting. | --- ### 🔀 Pull Request | Field | Details | |-------|---------| | **Title** | `docs: fix README setup typo and benchmark command wording` | | **Body** | • Fix typo in README setup instructions
• Clarify benchmark execution command
• Docs-only simulation benchmark output | | **PR URL** | [#128 → View Pull Request](https://github.com/octo-org/benchmark-demo-repo/pull/128) | --- The workflow executed in three steps: 1. **`getRepoTask()`** — Retrieved the repo (`octo-org/benchmark-demo-repo`), default branch (`main`), and the requested change. 2. **`applyRepoChange({ repo, branch })`** — Applied the change to the repository. 3. **Returned** the full PR metadata including changed files, PR title, body, and URL.

data.systemPrompt:
You are a specialised benchmark agent for Scenario 6.
You are now in code-mode.
You only know API method names at the start (no signatures or argument details yet).

Known API names: getRepoTask, applyRepoChange

You may call these tools:
1) search_api_definition(apiName) to fetch API docs for one method.
2) execute_scenario code executor tool to run TypeScript.

Rules:
- Use search_api_definition before using any API whose contract you are not fully sure about.
- Write TypeScript only.
- Do not output chain-of-thought.
- Keep responses short.

Write TypeScript and call execute_scenario6_code with key { typescript }. The code must return exactly { repo, branch, changedFiles, prTitle, prBody, prUrl }.
data.userPrompt:
Fetch a repository, apply a requested change, and create a pull request.
Return the changed files and PR metadata.
